name: Hornachuelos from Palma del Rio — circular by Posadas
name_original: Hornachuelos desde Palma del Río — circular por Posadas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Palma Del Río are situated in the fertile Vega del Guadalquivir, at the confluence of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers. This creates a landscape of lowlands characterized by extensive orange groves and riverside scenery. While much of the terrain is flat or gently rolling, some routes extend into areas with moderate to significant elevation gains, offering varied challenges for road cyclists. The region's natural features include river islands and the Embalse de Peñaflor reservoir. Best road cycling routes around Palma Del Río The most popular road cycling route is Hornachuelos from Palma del Rio — circular by Posadas, a 34.7 miles (55.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores the countryside towards Hornachuelos. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Palma del Rio to La Puebla de los Infantes – circular via Peñaflor, a moderate 31.9 miles (51.3 km) path. This route offers views of the agricultural landscape as it heads towards La Puebla de los Infantes and Peñaflor. Local road cyclists also love the Peñaflor – Retortillo Reservoir loop from Palma del Río, a 70.6 miles (113.7 km) trail leading through the wider region, often completed in about 5 hours 22 minutes. This difficult route includes substantial elevation gain. Road cycling around Palma Del Río is defined by fertile lowlands, extensive orange groves, and the confluence of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier rides through the plains to more challenging routes with significant ascents. The routes in Palma Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Palma Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Palma Del Río?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Palma Del Río, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy, flat rides to more challenging journeys with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Palma Del Río offers several easier routes. The region's fertile lowlands provide many flat or gently rolling paths perfect for beginners. For example, the "Hospital de San Sebastian – Plaza Mayor de Andalucía loop" mentioned in regional research is an easier 26.4 km option. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed in the provided data, the general terrain of the lowlands is conducive to less strenuous rides.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Palma Del Río?
A: Road cycling in Palma Del Río offers diverse landscapes. You'll primarily traverse the fertile Vega del Guadalquivir, characterized by extensive orange groves, especially prominent in the Santa Lucía valley. Routes also follow the confluence of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers, providing picturesque riverside scenery and views of river islands. Some routes extend to areas with more varied terrain and elevation.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Palma Del Río offers routes with significant ascents. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor – Retortillo Reservoir loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 113.7 km and includes substantial elevation gain. Another challenging option is the "Plaza Mayor de Andalucía – Viewpoint of Las Palomas loop," which is 60.8 km with an 800m elevation gain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the region's natural beauty. You can expect vistas of the vast orange groves, particularly in the Santa Lucía valley. The confluence of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers provides unique riverside scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2837075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Las Palomas&lt;/a&gt; offers impressive views, and the Embalse de Peñaflor (Peñaflor Reservoir) is another significant natural feature you might encounter. For more natural sights, you can explore &lt;a href="https://visitapalmadelrio.com/naturaleza/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palma del Río's nature&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites I can visit near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Palma Del Río is rich in cultural and historical points of interest. You might consider visiting the Palacio Portocarrero, an Alcázar Palace with Mudejar gardens, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7163453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hotel Monasterio de San Francisco&lt;/a&gt;. The town's historic center with its well-preserved walled enclosure is also worth exploring. The Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de Belén offers impressive views of the Vega and its orange groves.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Palma Del Río?
A: The spring and summer months are particularly appealing for road cycling in Palma Del Río. During this time, the orange groves are in bloom, filling the air with fragrance and creating a stunning 'sea of silver' landscape. The weather is generally favorable for outdoor activities, though summer can be quite warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides might be preferable.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Palma Del Río are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010641055/hornachuelos-from-palma-del-rio-circular-by-posadas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos from Palma del Rio — circular by Posadas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 55.8 km ride, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995740223/from-palma-del-rio-to-la-puebla-de-los-infantes-circular-via-penaflor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Palma del Rio to La Puebla de los Infantes – circular via Peñaflor&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 51.3 km path.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Palma Del Río?
A: The road cycling routes in Palma Del Río are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars. Over 300 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the picturesque orange groves, the unique riverside scenery, and the options for different ability levels.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while cycling in the region?
A: Given Palma Del Río's location at the confluence of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers, the area is known for its diverse birdlife. Cycling along the riverbanks or through the more natural areas may offer opportunities for bird spotting and observing other local fauna.

Q: Are there longer road cycling routes for endurance training?
A: For those looking for longer rides and endurance training, Palma Del Río offers routes that extend through the wider region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor – Retortillo Reservoir loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 113.7 km trail, typically completed in over 5 hours, and includes substantial elevation gain, making it ideal for longer training sessions.
tips: Swamp of the Bembezar river, located in the town of Hornachuelos, a beautiful town located inside the Sierra de Hornachuelos.

A small viewpoint where we can see the Bembézar River that feeds the reservoir of the same name. Hornachuelos is a town in the Sierra Morena, where we can get our water supply from one of the public fountains, before continuing on our route.

Old bridge that connects Palma del Río with the train station. From here there are good views of the new bridge over the Guadalquivir river built in 2007 - Puente Reina Sofía - and its pretty arch silhouette.
